prose:
Method-provenance block
Property: Condensed Fukui reactivity descriptors (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor for nitrobenzene; headline value reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index, f⁺ = 0.4964178 for atom 4 (C).
Level of theory: DFT tier "standard" = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RIJK density-fitting.
Geometry provenance: single-point calculations on the user-supplied geometry (file: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz); no re-optimization at DFT (notation: Fukui//input geometry).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0, 1) for the N-electron state; cation (+1, 2) and anion (–1, 2) states were also evaluated to obtain the ±1 electron densities.
Software/backend: PySCF (via chemkit fukui-reactivity skill, B3LYP/def2-TZVP).
Convergence & checks: SCF cycles converged for all three charge states. integrity.status = "ok"; integrity.trustworthy = true. The built-in charge-conservation checks passed (sum(f⁺) = 1.0000; sum(f⁻) = 1.0000).
Warnings: no warnings reported in the tool’s `warnings` array.
Live log & files: computation ran via
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --multiplicity 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpc1agndb7/live.json
The engine’s stdout/stderr streamed during the run to the usual chemkit live log `fukui_<timestamp>.out` in the caller’s working directory; the full JSON result is at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpc1agndb7/live.json.

Key results (Mulliken-condensed indices):
• Most electrophilic site: atom 4 (C, index 3) f⁺ = 0.4964178
• Most nucleophilic site: atom 5 (C, index 4) f⁻ = 0.1072882
• Dual descriptor (f⁺ – f⁻) largest magnitude at atom 4 (0.51070984)

The headline value returned below is the maximal electrophilic Fukui index f⁺.

All quantities are dimensionless indices. No experimental comparison requested.
